transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` — bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` — dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN
Scenario seed: 12371812672373030993

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. In workspace `ws-0`, edit the following files (any content is fine; the test cares about coordination): shared/file-0.txt, ws-0/file-1.txt, ws-0/file-2.txt
3. In workspace `ws-0`, edit the following files (any content is fine; the test cares about coordination): ws-0/file-0.txt, ws-0/file-1.txt
4. Commit pending changes in workspace `ws-0` with a descriptive message.
transcript.convention_text:
# maw arm crib (current v2 `ws/` layout)

You coordinate multi-agent work using **maw**, a workspace-management tool built on git. The repo is laid out as a bare-style root containing `.git/` and a `ws/` directory; every workspace is an isolated git worktree under `ws/<name>/`. The default integration target lives at `ws/default/`. The `maw` binary is on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `maw ws create <name>` — make a new workspace (worktree) at `ws/<name>/`.
- `maw ws list` — list all workspaces with their state (active / stale / conflicted).
- `maw ws sync` — bring the current workspace up to the latest epoch (rebases workspace commits onto integration head). Use when you see `stale` in `maw ws list`.
- `maw ws diff <name>` — show the workspace's changes vs the epoch base.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --check` — dry-run a merge; reports conflicts without changing state.
- `maw ws merge <a> [b ...] --into default --destroy` — merge workspace(s) into default and destroy the source(s) on success.
- `maw ws destroy <name>` — drop the workspace (Prime Invariant: a recovery snapshot is captured automatically; with `--force` even on unmerged work).
- `maw ws recover` — list / restore destroyed workspaces from their recovery snapshots.
- `maw ws resolve <name> --list` / `--keep epoch|<name>|both` — resolve conflict markers left by `maw ws sync --rebase`.
- `maw doctor` — substrate-health probe.

## Running commands inside a workspace

The sandbox does not persist `cd` between tool calls. Use `maw exec <workspace> -- <cmd>` to run any command inside a workspace:

```
maw exec alice -- git status
maw exec alice -- git add -A
maw exec alice -- git commit -m "feat: …"
```

## Conflicts are data, not errors

- `maw ws sync --rebase` does not abort on conflict — it commits the marker-laden file, records structured conflict metadata under `.manifold/`, and continues. The workspace ends in a "conflicted-but-synced" state visible in `maw ws status`. Resolve via `maw ws resolve`.
- `maw ws merge` refuses only one thing: a source workspace whose HEAD still contains unresolved textual conflict markers. Pass `--force` only when markers are legitimate content (test fixtures, docs).

## Prime Invariant

No committed work is ever lost. `maw ws destroy` always captures a recovery snapshot under `refs/manifold/recovery/<name>/`; `maw ws recover` can list, inspect, search, and restore the contents of any destroyed workspace. If you suspect work was lost, run `maw ws recover` before reopening the task.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `maw --help` and `maw ws --help` for the full surface.
- `maw doctor` if anything looks broken before you start.

turns.reply_text: I'll complete this task battery step by step. Let me start by checking the workspace state and then create the required workspace.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
maw doctor
==========

[OK] git: git version 2.54.0
[OK] git version: 2.54.0 (>= 2.40.0)
[OK] manifold metadata: .manifold/ exists
[OK] default workspace: ws/default/ exists with source files
[OK] lfs: no LFS tracked patterns
[OK] repo root: bare (no source files)
[OK] legacy jj metadata: none
[OK] dangling snapshots: none
[OK] abandoned-with-snapshot: no queued recovery snapshots
[OK] stale head refs: none
[OK] merge-state: no merge in progress
[OK] epoch drift: in sync (027d4d3f76c7 == 'main')
[OK] git HEAD: ref: refs/heads/main

All checks passed!

turns.reply_text: Now I'll create the `ws-0` workspace and edit the required files:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create ws-0","description":"Create workspace ws-0 based on default branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Workspace create requires an explicit source.
  Use one of:
    maw ws create --from main ws-0
    maw ws create --change <change-id> ws-0

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw ws create --from main ws-0","description":"Create workspace ws-0 from main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Creating workspace 'ws-0' at ws/ws-0 ...

Workspace 'ws-0' ready!

  Mode:   ephemeral
  Epoch:  027d4d3f76c7 (base commit for this workspace)
  Path:   /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0/

  IMPORTANT: All file reads, writes, and edits must use this path.
  This is your working directory for ALL operations, not just bash.

To start working:

  # Edit files under /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0/
  # Changes are detected automatically by the merge engine

  # Run commands in the workspace:
  maw exec ws-0 -- cargo test
  maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy

Note: All edits in the workspace are tracked automatically.
The merge engine captures changes when merging.

turns.reply_text: Now let me check what files exist in the workspace and then edit the required files:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"find /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0 -type f -not -path '*/.git/*' | head -20","description":"List files in ws-0 workspace"}

turns.reply_text: I'll now create and edit the required files in the workspace. Let me create each file:

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0/shared/file-0.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0/ws-0/file-1.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0/ws-0/file-2.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_calls.name: Write
tool_calls.result_truncated: File created successfully at: /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0/ws-0/file-0.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

turns.reply_text: Now I need to edit file-1.txt in the second batch as required by the task:

tool_calls.name: Edit
tool_calls.args_json: {"file_path":"/tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0/ws-0/file-1.txt","new_string":"ws-0 file 1 content (edited in second batch)","old_string":"ws-0 file 1 content","replace_all":false}
tool_calls.result_truncated: The file /tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0/ws-0/file-1.txt has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

turns.reply_text: Now I'll verify the changes and commit them to the workspace:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git status","description":"Check git status in ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Not currently on any branch.
Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
	shared/
	ws-0/

nothing added to commit but untracked files present (use "git add" to track)

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json:
{"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git add -A && git commit -m "Edit shared and workspace-specific files

- shared/file-0.txt: add shared content
- ws-0/file-0.txt: add workspace-specific content
- ws-0/file-1.txt: add and edit workspace-specific content
- ws-0/file-2.txt: add workspace-specific content"","description":"Stage and commit all changes"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 128
fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree

turns.reply_text: Let me check the git state more carefully:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git rev-parse --show-toplevel","description":"Check git top-level"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git log --oneline -3","description":"Check recent commits"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git -c user.email=\"test@example.com\" -c user.name=\"Test User\" add -A","description":"Stage all files"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json:
{"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git -c user.email="test@example.com" -c user.name="Test User" commit -m "Edit shared and workspace-specific files

- shared/file-0.txt: add shared content
- ws-0/file-0.txt: add workspace-specific content
- ws-0/file-1.txt: add and edit workspace-specific content
- ws-0/file-2.txt: add workspace-specific content"","description":"Commit all changes"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
[detached HEAD d2b7388] Edit shared and workspace-specific files
 4 files changed, 4 insertions(+)
 create mode 100644 shared/file-0.txt
 create mode 100644 ws-0/file-0.txt
 create mode 100644 ws-0/file-1.txt
 create mode 100644 ws-0/file-2.txt

turns.reply_text: Perfect! The commit was successful. Let me verify the final state:

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"maw exec ws-0 -- git log --oneline -2","description":"Verify commit was created"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
d2b7388 Edit shared and workspace-specific files
027d4d3 init

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default	/tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/default
ws-0	/tmp/.tmpaKvfIN/ws/ws-0 (ready to merge) [lifecycle:committed-unintegrated]

Merge ready: maw ws merge ws-0 --into default --destroy

turns.reply_text:
done

All tasks completed successfully:

1. ✅ Created workspace `ws-0` from main branch
2. ✅ Edited files in first batch: shared/file-0.txt, ws-0/file-1.txt, ws-0/file-2.txt
3. ✅ Edited files in second batch: ws-0/file-0.txt, ws-0/file-1.txt (updated)
4. ✅ Committed all changes with descriptive message

The workspace is now ready to merge (status shows "ready to merge"). All edits have been tracked and committed as required.
